embedding query: actor: All AI systems text: Applies to: All AI systems.

top matches.text: The operator shall ensure that all appropriate corrective action is taken in respect of all the AI systems concerned that it has made available on the Union market.

High-risk AI systems shall comply with the requirements laid down in this Section, taking into account High - risk AI systems' intended purpose as well as the generally acknowledged state of the art on AI and AI-related technologies.
top matches.embedding text: actions: comply action: comply object: with the requirements laid down in this Section, taking into account High - risk AI systems' intended purpose as well as the generally acknowledged state of the art on AI and AI-related technologies. actor: High-risk AI systems modal: Mandatory(shall) text: High-risk AI systems shall comply with the requirements laid down in this Section, taking into account High - risk AI systems' intended purpose as well as the generally acknowledged state of the art on AI and AI-related technologies.

top matches.text: Particular attention shall be given to AI systems presenting a risk to vulnerable groups.

AI systems presenting a risk shall be understood as a 'product presenting a risk' as defined in Article 3, point 19 of Regulation (EU) 2019/1020, in so far as they present risks to the health or safety, or to fundamental rights, of persons.

High-risk AI systems shall be resilient against attempts by unauthorised third parties to alter High - risk AI systems' use, outputs or performance by exploiting system vulnerabilities. The technical solutions aiming to ensure the cybersecurity of high-risk AI systems shall be appropriate to the relevant circumstances and the risks. The technical solutions to address AI specific vulnerabilities shall include, where appropriate, measures to prevent, detect, respond to, resolve and control for attacks trying to manipulate the training data set (data poisoning), or pre-trained components used in training (model poisoning), inputs designed to cause the AI model to make a mistake (adversarial examples or model evasion), confidentiality attacks or model flaws.
top matches.embedding text: actions: be resilient action: be resilient object: against attempts by unauthorised third parties to alter High - risk AI systems' use, outputs or performance by exploiting system vulnerabilities. The technical solutions aiming to ensure the cybersecurity of high-risk AI systems shall be appropriate to the relevant circumstances and the risks. The technical solutions aiming to ensure the cybersecurity of high-risk AI systems shall be appropriate to the relevant circumstances and the risks. The technical solutions to address AI specific vulnerabilities shall include, where appropriate, measures to prevent, detect, respond to, resolve and control for attacks trying to manipulate the training data set (data poisoning), or pre-trained components used in training (model poisoning), inputs designed to cause the AI model to make a mistake (adversarial examples or model evasion), confidentiality attacks or model flaws.

top matches.text: Deployers of high-risk AI systems shall take appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure they use such systems in accordance with the instructions for use accompanying the systems, pursuant to paragraphs 3 and 6.
High-risk AI systems shall technically allow for the automatic recording of events (logs) over the lifetime of the system.
top matches.embedding text: actions: technically action: technically object: allow for the automatic recording of events (logs) over the lifetime of the system. actor: High-risk AI systems modal: Mandatory(shall) text: High-risk AI systems shall technically allow for the automatic recording of events (logs) over the lifetime of the system.

top matches.text: High-risk AI systems shall be as resilient as possible regarding errors, faults The robustness of high-risk AI systems may be achieved through technical redundancy solutions, which may include backup or fail-safe plans. High-risk AI systems that continue to learn after being placed on the market or put into service shall be developed in such a way as to eliminate or reduce as far as possible the risk of possibly biased outputs influencing input for future operations (feedback loops), and as to ensure that any such feedback loops are duly addressed with appropriate mitigation measures.

top matches.text: The testing of high-risk AI systems shall be performed, as appropriate, at any time throughout the development process, and, in any event, prior to The testing of high - risk AI systems' being placed on the market or put into service.

top matches.text: For high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1 (a), of Annex III, the logging capabilities shall provide, at a minimum: (a) recording of the period of each use of the system (start date and time and end date and time of each use);

top matches.text: The Deployer shall ensure the proper functioning of that AI system, including as regards software updates; The Deployer shall use deployers appropriately to interpret the output of the high-risk AI system where applicable.

top matches.text: The Organization SHALL establish, implement, document, and maintain a risk management system in relation to high-risk AI systems.

top matches.text: For high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1 (a), of Annex III, the logging capabilities shall provide, at a minimum: (b) the reference database against which input data has been checked by the system;

top matches.text: Where a product contains an AI system, to which the requirements of this Regulation as well as requirements of the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Section A of Annex I apply, providers shall be responsible for ensuring that a product's product is fully compliant with all applicable requirements under applicable Union harmonisation legislation.
top matches.embedding text: actions: be responsible action: be responsible object: for ensuring that a product's product is fully compliant with all applicable requirements under applicable Union harmonisation legislation. actor: providers modal: Mandatory(shall) condition: Where a product contains an AI system text: Where a product contains an AI system, to which the requirements of this Regulation as well as requirements of the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Section A of Annex I apply, providers shall be responsible for ensuring that a product's product is fully compliant with all applicable requirements under applicable Union harmonisation legislation.

top matches.text: Where the operator of an AI system does not take adequate corrective action within the period referred to in paragraph 2, the market surveillance authority shall take all appropriate provisional measures to prohibit or restrict the AI system's being made available on its national market or put into service, to withdraw the product or the standalone AI system from that market or to recall it.
Testing shall ensure that high-risk AI systems perform consistently for Testing's intended purpose and that Testing are in compliance with the requirements set out in this Section.
top matches.embedding text: actions: ensure action: ensure object: that high-risk AI systems perform consistently for Testing's intended purpose and that Testing are in compliance with the requirements set out in this Section. actor: Testing modal: Mandatory(shall) text: Testing shall ensure that high-risk AI systems perform consistently for Testing's intended purpose and that Testing are in compliance with the requirements set out in this Section.

top matches.text: deployer High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way as to ensure that High - risk AI systems' operation is sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret a system's output and use such a way appropriately.

top matches.text: The Provider SHALL draw up technical documentation of a high-risk AI system before that system is placed on the market. The Provider SHALL draw up technical documentation in such a way as to demonstrate that the high-risk AI system complies with the requirements set out in this Section and to provide national competent authorities and notified bodies with the necessary information in a clear and comprehensive form to assess the compliance of the AI system with those requirements. A high-risk AI system SHALL contain, at a minimum, the elements set out in Annex IV. SMEs, including start-ups, MAY provide the elements of the technical documentation specified in Annex IV in a simplified manner. To that end, the Commission SHALL establish a simplified technical documentation form targeted at the needs of small and microenterprises. Where an SME, including a start-up, opts to provide the information required in Annex IV in a simplified manner, a high-risk AI system SHALL use the form referred to in this paragraph. Notified bodies SHALL accept the form for the purposes of the conformity assessment.

top matches.text: Where the market surveillance authority of a Member State has sufficient reason to consider an AI system to present a risk as referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, it shall carry out an evaluation of the AI system concerned in respect of its compliance with all the requirements and obligations laid down in this Regulation.

The risks referred to in this Article shall concern those which may be reasonably mitigated or eliminated through the development or design of the high-risk AI system, or the provision of adequate technical information.

top matches.text: Testing shall be carried out against prior defined metrics and probabilistic thresholds that are appropriate to the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system.
top matches.embedding text: actions: carried action: carried object: out against prior defined metrics and probabilistic thresholds that are appropriate to the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system. actor: Testing modal: Mandatory(shall) text: Testing shall be carried out against prior defined metrics and probabilistic thresholds that are appropriate to the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system.

top matches.text: Providers or prospective providers may conduct testing of high-risk AI systems referred to in Annex III in real world conditions at any time before the placing on the market or the putting into service of the AI system on their own or in partnership with one or more deployers or prospective deployers.

Where applicable, deployers of high-risk AI systems shall use the information provided under Article 13 of this Regulation to comply with their obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment under Article 35 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 or Article 27 of Directive (EU) 2016/680.

The risk management system shall be understood as a continuous iterative process planned and run throughout the entire lifecycle of a high-risk AI system, requiring regular systematic review and updating.
top matches.embedding text: actions: be understood action: be understood object: as a continuous iterative process planned and run throughout the entire lifecycle of a high-risk AI system, requiring regular systematic review and updating. actor: The risk management system modal: Mandatory(shall) temporal: continuous iterative process planned and run text: The risk management system shall be understood as a continuous iterative process planned and run throughout the entire lifecycle of a high-risk AI system, requiring regular systematic review and updating.

top matches.text: They shall have the appropriate statistical properties, including, where applicable, as regards the persons or groups of persons in relation to whom the high-risk AI system is intended to be used.

top matches.text: The Authority SHALL test high-risk AI systems for the purpose of identifying the most appropriate and targeted risk management measures.
top matches.embedding text: actions: test action: test object: high-risk AI systems for the purpose of identifying the most appropriate and targeted risk management measures. actor: The Authority modal: Mandatory(shall) text: The Authority SHALL test high-risk AI systems for the purpose of identifying the most appropriate and targeted risk management measures.

top matches.text: For high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1(a) of Annex III, the measures referred to in paragraph 3 of this Article shall be such as to ensure that, in addition, no action or decision is taken by the deployer on the basis of the identification resulting from the system unless that identification has been separately verified and confirmed by at least two natural persons with the necessary competence, training and authority. The requirement for a separate verification by at least two natural persons shall not apply to high-risk AI systems used for the purposes of law enforcement, migration, border control or asylum, where Union or national law considers the application of this requirement to be disproportionate.
top matches.embedding text: actions: apply action: apply object: to high-risk AI systems used for the purposes of law enforcement, migration, border control or asylum, where Union or national law considers the application of this requirement to be disproportionate. actor: training and authority. top matches.text: Deployers of high-risk AI systems that are public authorities, or Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies shall comply with the registration obligations referred to in Article 49.

top matches.text: Deployers shall cooperate with the relevant competent authorities in any action those authorities take in relation to the high-risk AI system in order to implement this Regulation.

Before putting into service or using a high-risk AI system at the workplace, deployers who are employers shall inform workers' representatives and the affected workers that they will be subject to the use of the high-risk AI system.

When implementing the risk management system as provided for in paragraphs 1 to 7, providers shall give consideration to whether in view of the risk management system's intended purpose the high-risk AI system is likely to have an adverse impact on persons under the age of 18 and, as appropriate, other vulnerable groups.
